com.hp.workpath.api.config

Class ConfigService

  • java.lang.Object
    • com.hp.workpath.api.config.ConfigService

  • public final class ConfigService
extends java.lang.Object

    ConfigService provides interfaces to access an application configuration for retrieving or updating detail. If an application is installed as hpk file on a Workpath device, client can access the application configuration data without launching it.

    Since:
    API 1

    • Nested Class Summary

      Nested Classes 
      Modifier and Type Class and Description
      static class  ConfigService.AbstractConfigChangeObserver
      Provides a mechanism to monitor the state of the ConfigService operations.

    • Method Summary

      All Methods Static Methods Concrete Methods 
      Modifier and Type Method and Description
      static org.json.JSONObject getDefaultConfig(android.content.Context context, Result result)
      Retrieves configuration data that an application owns.
      static boolean isSupported(android.content.Context context)
      This method is needed to determine if the service is supported or not.
      static Result setDefaultConfig(android.content.Context context, org.json.JSONObject config)
      Updates configuration data that an application owns.

    • Method Detail

      • isSupported

        public static boolean isSupported(android.content.Context context)

        This method is needed to determine if the service is supported or not. If it's not supported, ConfigService operation will be failed.

        Parameters:
        context - The Context in which the application is running.
        Returns:
        boolean Returns result of supported. If service is supported, method returns true. Otherwise method returns false.
        Throws:
        java.lang.NullPointerException - Calling a method on a null reference(context) or trying to access a field of a null reference will trigger a NullPointerException.
        Since:
        API 1

      • getDefaultConfig

        public static org.json.JSONObject getDefaultConfig(android.content.Context context,
                                                   Result result)

        Retrieves configuration data that an application owns.

        Parameters:
        context - The Context in which the application is running. If it's null, configuration will not be retrieved.
        result - (optional) Indicates any errors which occurred while retrieving configuration.
        Returns:
        JSONObject Configuration data of an application
        Throws:
        java.lang.NullPointerException - Calling a method on a null reference(context) or trying to access a field of a null reference will trigger a NullPointerException.
        Since:
        API 1

      • setDefaultConfig

        public static Result setDefaultConfig(android.content.Context context,
                                      org.json.JSONObject config)

        Updates configuration data that an application owns.

        Parameters:
        context - The Context in which the application is running. If it's null, configuration will not be updated.
        config - The data for updating configuration of an application
        Returns:
        Result The result of the update
        Throws:
        java.lang.NullPointerException - If context is null or configuration is not provided.
        Since:
        API 1
